title: T-008 — shift_aggregator_v1 DSL rule registration + worker handler (OEE §7.1, D-OEE-2)
prompt:
# T-008 — shift_aggregator_v1 DSL rule

PRD: docs/prd/15-OEE-PRD.md §7.1 (`shift_aggregator_v1` registry entry, P1 active), §8.4 (post-shift-end flow), §11 V-OEE-AGG-1..3, D-OEE-2
Cross-PRD: docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md §7.8 (rule_definitions registry)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Register `shift_aggregator_v1` rule in 02-SETTINGS §7.8 rule_definitions registry AND implement its worker handler. The handler fires 5 min after each `shift_configs.end_time` per tenant (V-OEE-AGG-1), UPSERTs into `oee_shift_metrics` via CONCURRENT MV refresh OR a parallel materialised insert path (PRD §7.1 spec is the latter — see implementation contract), and emits `oee.shift.aggregated` outbox event (T-010 owns publisher; this task only writes the outbox row).

## Pre-flight
Read: `packages/db/migrations/` for latest migration number; existing `apps/worker/src/` structure (Wave1 T-111/T-112); `rule_definitions` table (02-SETTINGS); T-006 oee_shift_metrics MV exists; T-003 shift_configs exists; T-004 org_non_production_days exists (excluded days must be skipped per V-OEE-SHIFT spec).

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/NNNN_oee_b_shift_aggregator_v1.sql`: INSERT into rule_definitions (rule_id='shift_aggregator_v1', version='v1', phase='P1', active=TRUE, schedule_kind='post_shift_end_plus_5min', payload_schema JSONB) — idempotent ON CONFLICT.
2. Worker handler `apps/worker/src/oee/shift-aggregator-v1.ts`: for each (org, shift) due at `now() ≥ shift_end_time + 5min` AND `date NOT IN org_non_production_days`, perform UPSERT against oee_shift_metrics (treat the MV as a target — use REFRESH CONCURRENTLY because the MV is the canonical store; PRD §7.1 alternative INSERT into a parallel table is OUT of P1 scope unless the MV refresh is unworkable, in which case escalate). The handler MUST be idempotent (V-OEE-AGG-2): re-running for the same (org, line, shift, shift_date) is a no-op. Empty shifts (snapshot_count=0) are skipped (V-OEE-AGG-3).
3. After successful refresh per (org, shift_date, shift_id) tuple, write one row to the outbox table (defined in T-010) with event_type='oee.shift.aggregated' and payload {org_id, site_id, line_id, shift_id, shift_date, oee_pct, availability_pct, performance_pct, quality_pct, total_output_qty, total_downtime_min}.
4. Wire to Wave1 T-111 worker loop: handler is registered via the standard tenant-scoped worker contract (per-org loop via T-112).
5. Observability: every handler run emits a trace span (Wave1 T-117) and structured log (T-116) tagged with rule_id, org_id, shift_id, shift_date, snapshot_count, refresh_ms.
6. Vitest covering: (a) registration row exists, (b) idempotent re-run (V-OEE-AGG-2), (c) empty-shift skip (V-OEE-AGG-3), (d) 5-min post-shift-end gate (V-OEE-AGG-1), (e) excluded date (org_non_production_days) skipped, (f) outbox row written exactly once per (org, line, shift, date) tuple, (g) trace span emitted (assert via Wave1 tracer test harness).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_oee_b_shift_aggregator_v1.sql [create]
- apps/worker/src/oee/shift-aggregator-v1.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/oee/__tests__/shift-aggregator-v1.test.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/registry.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs, when `SELECT * FROM rule_definitions WHERE rule_id='shift_aggregator_v1'` runs, then exactly one active=TRUE row with phase='P1' is returned (idempotent re-run keeps row count=1).
2. Given seeded snapshots for shift AM ending at 08:00 UTC and a worker tick at 08:05, when the handler runs, then oee_shift_metrics has the AM row UPSERTed and a single outbox row with event_type='oee.shift.aggregated' is written; given the handler runs again at 08:06 for the same tuple, then no additional outbox row is written and oee_shift_metrics row is unchanged (V-OEE-AGG-2 idempotency).
3. Given an org_non_production_days row for the shift's date, when the handler runs for that date, then no MV refresh and no outbox row are produced.
4. Given a shift with snapshot_count=0 in oee_snapshots, when the handler runs, then no oee_shift_metrics row is materialised and no outbox row is written (V-OEE-AGG-3).
5. Given the handler completes, when traces are inspected (Wave1 T-117 harness), then a span tagged with rule_id, org_id, shift_id, shift_date, snapshot_count, refresh_ms is present.

## Test strategy
- RED: vitest oee-shift-aggregator-v1 asserting five ACs; uses fake-time helper to simulate now() vs shift end + 5 min.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker vitest run oee/shift-aggregator-v1`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not run the aggregator synchronously in the request path (D-OEE-1).
- Do not fire before shift_end_time + 5 min — V-OEE-AGG-1 (data integrity for late events).
- Do not write idempotency on (line, shift) only — include shift_date in idempotency key.
- Do not bypass observability — every handler run emits trace + structured log (Wave1 T-116/T-117).
- Do not register pg_cron — Wave1 T-111 worker owns scheduling.

pipeline_inputs.description: Register shift_aggregator_v1 in 02-SETTINGS rule registry + implement worker handler with 5-min post-shift gate, idempotent UPSERT, non-production-day skip, outbox emission, traces.
pipeline_inputs.details: Wave1 T-111 worker owns scheduling — handler is dispatched per-tenant via T-112 loop. Idempotency key = (org_id, line_id, shift_id, shift_date). Outbox row consumed by T-010 publisher → 12-REPORTING (cache bust) + 13-MAINTENANCE (D-MNT-3 MTBF/MTTR feed). Observability traces via Wave1 T-117 + structured logs via T-116. Cross-module: depends on org_non_production_days (T-004) to skip closure days.
out_of_scope: Anomaly detector v1 / maintenance trigger v1 (T-023 stubs)
acceptance_criteria: Given migration runs, when SELECT FROM rule_definitions WHERE rule_id='shift_aggregator_v1' runs, then exactly one active=TRUE row with phase='P1' is returned and re-run keeps row count=1

Given seeded snapshots for shift AM and worker tick at shift_end_time + 5 min, when handler runs, then oee_shift_metrics is UPSERTed and exactly one outbox row event_type='oee.shift.aggregated' is written; re-run at +6 min is a no-op (V-OEE-AGG-2)
Given org_non_production_days for the shift date, when handler runs, then no MV refresh and no outbox row are produced
Given a shift with snapshot_count=0, when handler runs, then no MV row is materialised and no outbox row is written (V-OEE-AGG-3)

Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker vitest run oee/shift-aggregator-v1`
risk_red_lines: Do not run aggregator synchronously in request path
Do not fire before shift_end_time + 5 min (V-OEE-AGG-1)
Do not bypass shift_date in idempotency key
Do not bypass Wave1 T-116/T-117 observability
Do not register pg_cron — Wave1 T-111 worker
